beginning.label: Focus here first
beginning.consequence: There is lots to learn about using AI well. Start with the guidance below — it is designed to help, not judge.

developing.label: Keep building
developing.consequence: You are learning how to use AI — the tips below will help you build confidence, independence and safer habits.

emerging.label: Growing awareness
emerging.consequence: You show moderate reliance on AI and reasonable awareness. Small changes to how you use AI could strengthen your learning even further.

confident.label: Good awareness
confident.consequence: You have good AI awareness and healthy habits in most areas. Focus on the skills below where you can still grow.

advanced.label: Strong skills
advanced.consequence: You are building strong AI learning habits — keep verifying, thinking independently and protecting your privacy.

headline.beginning: Start with one safer AI habit: try first, check answers, and keep personal details out.
headline.developing: You are learning how to use AI well. A few stronger routines will protect your thinking.
headline.emerging: Your AI habits are growing. Keep practising independence, verification and privacy.
headline.confident: You have good AI learning habits. Keep checking and explaining answers in your own words.
headline.advanced: You are building strong AI study skills and can model responsible use for others.

critical.consequence: AI tools can produce unfair or stereotypical answers about groups of people. Learning to spot this is an important part of using AI safely.

high.consequence: You may not yet check whether AI answers are fair to everyone. This is a skill worth building — unfair AI outputs can harm people.

moderate.consequence: You have some awareness of AI bias but do not always check for unfair answers. Keep practising — it gets easier.

low.consequence: You understand that AI can be unfair and think about this when you use it. Keep checking as you encounter new tools and topics.

emerging.summary: You may be using AI before giving yourself a real chance to think. The best students use AI to check and improve their thinking — not to replace it. Your ideas are the part AI can't do for you.
actions: Try the task yourself first — even if your answer feels incomplete
Use AI to check or improve what you wrote, not to write it for you
Ask your teacher to explain anything you don't understand rather than asking AI

developing.summary: You sometimes reach for AI before attempting work yourself. Build a habit of trying first — then use AI to strengthen your thinking.
actions: Try the task yourself first — even if your answer feels incomplete
Use AI to check or improve what you wrote, not to write it for you

emerging.summary: You may not always think about what information is safe to share with AI tools. Anything you type into a public AI tool could be stored and used by the company that runs it.
actions: Keep personal details out of AI tools — use "a student" not your name
Only use AI tools your school has approved
If something feels wrong or uncomfortable, tell a teacher

developing.summary: You have some privacy awareness but may still share more than you should. Be careful about personal details in public AI tools.
actions: Keep personal details out of AI tools — use "a student" not your name
Only use AI tools your school has approved

emerging.summary: Checking AI answers before you trust them is a skill worth building. AI can sound confident even when it is wrong.
actions: Check AI answers against trusted sources
Explain the answer in your own words
If you cannot explain it yourself, you may not fully understand it yet

emerging.summary: AI tools can produce answers that are unfair or stereotypical about groups of people. Learning to spot this helps you use AI more safely — and helps protect others.
actions: Ask: "Could this answer upset or exclude someone?"
Compare AI answers with what you learn in school about fairness and respect
Tell a teacher if you see AI content that seems discriminatory

developing.summary: You are starting to think about fairness in AI answers. Keep practising — checking for bias becomes a habit over time.
actions: Question answers that sound like stereotypes
Use trusted sources to check facts about people and cultures
